title: Surface area using nets
description: Practice using the net of a 3D figure to find surface area.

assessment_items.question_content:
Check out this cube:


[[☃ image 2]]

**Find the surface area of the cube (above) using its net (below).**  

[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ input-number 1]] units$^2$
hints:
All  $6$ of the faces are squares.

>$\begin{align} \text{Area of a square} &=  \text{side} \cdot \text{side}\\\\
&= 2 \cdot 2\\\\
&= \blueD{4}\\\\
\end{align}$

>$\begin{align} \text{Surface area of a cube} &=  6 \cdot \blueD{\text{Area of a square}}\\\\
&= 6 \cdot \blueD{4}\\\\
&= 24
\end{align}$
**The surface area of this cube is $24$ units$^2$.**

options.alt: A net on a coordinate grid.  The net consists of 6 squares. All of the squares have side lengths of 2 units. 4 of the squares are are in a row. There is a square connected to the top and a square connected to the bottom of the second square, each with side lengths of 2 units.
A cube with side lengths of 2 units.

assessment_items.question_content:
Check out this cube:

[[☃ image 1]]

**Find the surface area of the cube (above) using its net (below).**  

[[☃ image 2]]

[[☃ input-number 1]] units$^2$
hints:
All  $6$ of the faces are squares.

>$\begin{align} \text{Area of a square} &=  \text{side} \cdot \text{side}\\\\
&= 3\cdot3\\\\
&= \blueD{9}\\\\
\end{align}$

>$\begin{align} \text{Surface area of a cube} &=  6 \cdot \blueD{\text{Area of a square}}\\\\
&= 6 \cdot \blueD{9}\\\\
&= 54
\end{align}$
**The surface area of this cube is $54$ units$^2$.**

options.alt: The figure presents a cube. The length of one edge is labeled as 3 units

The figure presents a surface net of a cube. The net consists of 4 squares connected in a row. The second square from the left is also connected to a square above it, and a square below it. The length of one side of one square is labeled as 3 units.

assessment_items.question_content:
Check out this rectangular prism

[[☃ image 5]]

**Find the surface area of the rectangular prism (above) using its net (below).**  

[[☃ image 6]]

[[☃ input-number 1]] units$^2$
hints:
Each of the sides is a $5$ by $3$ rectangle. 

>$\begin{align} \text{Area of a rectangle} &=  \text{length} \cdot \text{width}\\\\
&= 5 \cdot 3\\\\
&= {15} \\\\
\end{align}$

The total area of the sides  is $2 \cdot 15 = \blueD{30}$.

The bottom and the top are both $5$ by $2$ rectangles.

>$\begin{align} \text{Area of a rectangle} &=  \text{length} \cdot \text{width}\\\\
&= 5 \cdot 2\\\\
&= {10} \\\\
\end{align}$

The total area of the bottom and the top is $2 \cdot 10 = \greenD{20}$.

The back and the front are both $3$ by $2$ rectangles.


>$\begin{align} \text{Area of a rectangle} &=  \text{length} \cdot \text{width}\\\\
&= 3 \cdot 2\\\\
&= {6} \\\\
\end{align}$

The total area of the back and the front  is $2 \cdot 6 = \goldD{12}$.

Let's add the areas we found to find the surface area.

>$\begin{align} \text{Surface area} &= \blueD{30}+ \greenD{20} + \goldD{12} \\\\
&= 62\\\\
\end{align}$
**The surface area of this rectangular prism is $62$ units$^2$.**

options.alt: The figure presents a rectangular prism. The line connecting the left and right faces is labeled as 2 units. The line connecting the top and bottom faces is labeled as 3 units. The line connecting the front and back faces is labeled as 5 units.

The figure presents a surface net of a rectangular prism. The net consists of 4 rectangles in a row, where the second rectangle from the left is also connected to a rectangle above it and a rectangle below it. The bottom of the left most rectangle is labeled as 3 units. The bottom of the second rectangle from the left is labeled as 2 units. The right side of the second rectangle from the left is labeled as 5 units. The left side of the rectangle located beneath the second rectangle is labeled as 3 units.

assessment_items.question_content:
Check out this rectangular prism


[[☃ image 1]]

**Find the surface area of the rectangular prism (above) using its net (below).**  


[[☃ image 2]]

[[☃ input-number 2]] units$^2$
hints:
Each of the sides is a $2$ by $2$ square. 

>$\begin{align} \text{Area of a square} &=  \text{side} \cdot \text{side}\\\\
&= 2 \cdot 2\\\\
&= {4} \\\\
\end{align}$

The total area of the sides  is $2 \cdot 4 = \blueD{8}$.

The back, the front, the bottom and the top are all $2$ by $5$ rectangles.

>$\begin{align} \text{Area of a rectangle} &=  \text{length} \cdot \text{width}\\\\
&= 2 \cdot 5\\\\
&= {10} \\\\
\end{align}$

The total area of the back, the front, the bottom, and the top is $4 \cdot 10 = \greenD{40}$.

Let's add the areas we found to find the surface area.

>$\begin{align} \text{Surface area} &= \blueD{8}+ \greenD{40} \\\\
&= 48\\\\
\end{align}$
**The surface area of this rectangular prism is $48$ units$^2$.**

options.alt: A rectangular prism. The length is 5 units. The width is 2 units. The height is 2 units.

A net on a coordinate grid.  The net consists of 2 squares and 4 rectangles.  The 2 squares and 2 of the rectangles are connected by the short side.  The first square has side lengths of 2 units. The first rectangle has a base of 5 units and a height of 2 units. The second square has side lengths of 2 units. The second rectangle has a base of 5 units and a height of 2 units.  There is a rectangle connected to the top and a rectangle connected to the bottom of the second rectangle, each with a base of 5 units and a height of 2 units.

assessment_items.question_content:
Check out this rectangular prism


[[☃ image 1]]

**Find the surface area of the rectangular prism (above) using its net (below).**  


[[☃ image 2]]

[[☃ input-number 1]] units$^2$
hints:
Each of the sides is a $4$ by $2$ rectangle. 

>$\begin{align} \text{Area of a rectangle} &=  \text{length} \cdot \text{width}\\\\
&= 4 \cdot 2\\\\
&= {8} \\\\
\end{align}$

The total area of the sides  is $2 \cdot 8 = \blueD{16}$.

The bottom and the top are both $4$ by $3$ rectangles.

>$\begin{align} \text{Area of a rectangle} &=  \text{length} \cdot \text{width}\\\\
&= 4 \cdot 3\\\\
&= {12} \\\\
\end{align}$

The total area of the bottom and the top is $2 \cdot 12 = \greenD{24}$.

The back and the front are both $2$ by $3$ rectangles.


>$\begin{align} \text{Area of a rectangle} &=  \text{length} \cdot \text{width}\\\\
&= 2 \cdot 3\\\\
&= {6} \\\\
\end{align}$

The total area of the back and the front  is $2 \cdot 6 = \goldD{12}$.

Let's add the areas we found to find the surface area.

>$\begin{align} \text{Surface area} &= \blueD{16}+ \greenD{24} + \goldD{12} \\\\
&= 52\\\\
\end{align}$
**The surface area of this rectangular prism is $52$ units$^2$.**

options.alt: A rectangular prism. The length is 3 units. The width is 4 units. The height is 2 units.

A net on a coordinate grid.  The net consists of 6 rectangles.  4 of the rectangles are connected by the long side.  The first rectangle has a base of 2 units and a height of 4 units.  The second rectangle has a base of 3 units and a height of 4 units. The third rectangle has a base of 2 units and a height of 4 units.  The fourth rectangle has a base of 3 units and a height of 4 units. There is a rectangle connected to the top and a rectangle connected to the bottom of the second rectangle, each with a base of 3 units and a height of 2 units.

assessment_items.question_content:
Check out this rectangular prism


[[☃ image 1]]

**Find the surface area of the rectangular prism (above) using its net (below).**  


[[☃ image 2]]



[[☃ input-number 1]] units$^2$
hints:
Let's completely label the net of the rectangular prism.

[[☃ image 1]]

Each of the sides is a $2$ by $3$ rectangle. 

>$\begin{align} \text{Area of a rectangle} &=  \text{length} \cdot \text{width}\\\\
&= 2 \cdot 3\\\\
&= {6} \\\\
\end{align}$

The total area of the sides  is $2 \cdot 6 = \blueD{12}$.

The bottom and the top are both $2$ by $7$ rectangles.

>$\begin{align} \text{Area of a rectangle} &=  \text{length} \cdot \text{width}\\\\
&= 2 \cdot 7\\\\
&= {14} \\\\
\end{align}$

The total area of the bottom and the top is $2 \cdot 14 = \greenD{28}$.

The back and the front are both $3$ by $7$ rectangles.


>$\begin{align} \text{Area of a rectangle} &=  \text{length} \cdot \text{width}\\\\
&= 3 \cdot 7\\\\
&= {21} \\\\
\end{align}$

The total area of the back and the front  is $2 \cdot 21 = \goldD{42}$.

Let's add the areas we found to find the surface area.

>$\begin{align} \text{Surface area} &= \blueD{12}+ \greenD{28} + \goldD{42} \\\\
&= 82\\\\
\end{align}$
**The surface area of this rectangular prism is $82$ units$^2$.**

options.alt: A rectangular prism. The length is 7 units. The width is 2 units. The height is 3 units.

A net on a coordinate grid.  The net consists of 6 rectangles.  4 of the rectangles are connected by the short side.  The first rectangle has a base of 3 units and a height of 2 units.  The second rectangle has a base of 7 units and a height of 2 units. The third rectangle has a base of 3 units and a height of 2 units.  The fourth rectangle has a base of 7 units and a height of 2 units. There is a rectangle connected to the top and a rectangle connected to the bottom of the second rectangle, each with a base of 7 units and a height of 3 units.

assessment_items.question_content:
Check out this triangular prism:



[[☃ image 1]]

**Find the surface area of the triangular prism (above) using its net (below).**  


[[☃ image 2]]

[[☃ input-number 1]] units$^2$
hints:
$2$ of the faces are $7$ by $5$ rectangles. 

>$\begin{align} \text{Area of a rectangle} &=  \text{length} \cdot \text{width}\\\\
&= 7 \cdot 5\\\\
&= {35} \\\\
\end{align}$

The total area of these $2$ rectangles  is $2 \cdot 35 = \blueD{70}$.

$1$ of the faces is a $7$ by $6$ rectangle.

>$\begin{align} \text{Area of a rectangle} &=  \text{length} \cdot \text{width}\\\\
&= 7 \cdot 6\\\\
&= {\greenD{42}} \\\\
\end{align}$

$2$ of the faces are triangles. Each triangle has the same base and height.

>$\begin{align} \text{Area of a triangle} &=  \dfrac12 \cdot \text{base} \cdot \text{height}\\\\
&= \dfrac12 \cdot 6 \cdot 4\\\\
&= 12 \\\\
\end{align}$


The total area of these $2$ triangles is $2 \cdot 12 = \goldD{24}$.

Let's add the areas we found to find the surface area.

>$\begin{align} \text{Surface area} &= \blueD{70}+ \greenD{42} + \goldD{24} \\\\
&= 136\\\\
\end{align}$
**The surface area of this triangular prism is $136$ units$^2$.**

options.alt: A triangular prism. The bottom face has side lengths of 6 units and 7 units. The other 2 faces have side lengths of 5 units and 7 units. The triangular prism has a height of 4 units.

A net on a coordinate grid.  The net consists of 3 rectangles and 2 triangles.  The rectangles are connected by the long side.  The first rectangle has a base of 5 units and a height of 7 units.  The second rectangle has a base of 6 units and a height of 7 units. The third rectangle has a base of 5 units and a height of 7 units.  There is a triangle connected to the top and bottom of the second rectangle.  Each right triangle has a base of 6 units and a height of 4 units.

assessment_items.question_content:
Check out this triangular prism:


[[☃ image 1]]

**Find the surface area of the triangular prism (above) using its net (below).**  


[[☃ image 2]]

[[☃ input-number 2]] units$^2$
hints:
$2$ of the faces are $7$ by $3$ rectangles. 

>$\begin{align} \text{Area of a rectangle} &=  \text{length} \cdot \text{width}\\\\
&= 7 \cdot 3\\\\
&= {21} \\\\
\end{align}$

The total area of these $2$ rectangles  is $2 \cdot 21 = \blueD{42}$.

$1$ of the faces is a $7$ by $6$ rectangle.

>$\begin{align} \text{Area of a rectangle} &=  \text{length} \cdot \text{width}\\\\
&= 7 \cdot 6\\\\
&= {\greenD{42}} \\\\
\end{align}$

$2$ of the faces are triangles. Each triangle has the same base and height.

>$\begin{align} \text{Area of a triangle} &=  \dfrac12 \cdot \text{base} \cdot \text{height}\\\\
&= \dfrac12 \cdot 6 \cdot 2\\\\
&= 6 \\\\
\end{align}$


The total area of these $2$ triangles is $2 \cdot 6 = \goldD{12}$.

Let's add the areas we found to find the surface area.

>$\begin{align} \text{Surface area} &= \blueD{42}+ \greenD{42} + \goldD{12} \\\\
&= 96\\\\
\end{align}$
**The surface area of this triangular prism is $96$ units$^2$.**

options.alt: A triangular prism. The bottom face has side lengths of 6 units and 7 units. The other 2 faces have side lengths of 3 units and 7 units. The triangular prism has a height of 2 units.

A net on a coordinate grid.  The net consists of 3 rectangles and 2 triangles.  The rectangles are connected by the long side.  The first rectangle has a base of 3 units and a height of 7 units.  The second rectangle has a base of 6 units and a height of 7 units. The third rectangle has a base of 3 units and a height of 7 units.  There is a triangle connected to the top and bottom of the second rectangle.  Each right triangle has a base of 6 units and a height of 2 units.

assessment_items.question_content:
Check out this right triangular prism:



[[☃ image 1]]

**Find the surface area of the right triangular prism (above) using its net (below).**  



[[☃ image 2]]

[[☃ input-number 2]] units$^2$
hints:
$1$ of the faces is an $11$ by $3$ rectangle.

>$\begin{align} \text{Area of a rectangle} &=  \text{length} \cdot \text{width}\\\\
&= 11 \cdot 3\\\\
&= {\blueD{33}} \\\\
\end{align}$

$1$ of the faces is an $11$ by $4$ rectangle.

>$\begin{align} \text{Area of a rectangle} &=  \text{length} \cdot \text{width}\\\\
&= 11 \cdot 4\\\\
&= {\greenD{44}} \\\\
\end{align}$

$1$ of the faces is an $11$ by $5$ rectangle.

>$\begin{align} \text{Area of a rectangle} &=  \text{length} \cdot \text{width}\\\\
&= 11 \cdot 5\\\\
&= {\goldD{55}} \\\\
\end{align}$

$2$ of the faces are triangles. Each triangle has the same base and height.

>$\begin{align} \text{Area of a triangle} &=  \dfrac12 \cdot \text{base} \cdot \text{height}\\\\
&= \dfrac12 \cdot 4 \cdot 3\\\\
&= 6 \\\\
\end{align}$


The total area of these $2$ triangles is $2 \cdot 6 = \maroonD{12}$.

Let's add the areas we found to find the surface area.

>$\begin{align} \text{Surface area} &= \blueD{33}+ \greenD{44} + \goldD{55} + \maroonD{12} \\\\
&= 144\\\\
\end{align}$
**The surface area of this triangular prism is $144$ units$^2$.**

options.alt: A right triangular prism. The bottom face has side lengths of 4 units and 11 units. The side face has side lengths of 3 units and 11 units. The diagonal side face has side lengths of 5 units and 11 units. The triangular prism has a height of 3 units.

A net on a coordinate grid.  The net consists of 3 rectangles and 2 right triangles.  The rectangles are connected by the long side.  The first rectangle has a base of 3 units and a height of 11 units.  The second rectangle has a base of 4 units and a height of 11 units. The third rectangle has a base of 5 units and a height of 11 units.  There is a right triangle connected to the top and bottom of the second rectangle.  Each right triangle has a base of 4 units and a height of 3 units.

assessment_items.question_content:
Check out this right triangular prism:

[[☃ image 1]]

**Find the surface area of the right triangular prism (above) using its net (below).**  



[[☃ image 2]]

[[☃ input-number 2]] units$^2$
hints:
$1$ of the faces is a $5$ by $7$ rectangle.

>$\begin{align} \text{Area of a rectangle} &=  \text{length} \cdot \text{width}\\\\
&= 5 \cdot 7\\\\
&= {\blueD{35}} \\\\
\end{align}$

$1$ of the faces is a $4$ by $7$ rectangle.

>$\begin{align} \text{Area of a rectangle} &=  \text{length} \cdot \text{width}\\\\
&= 4 \cdot 7\\\\
&= {\greenD{28}} \\\\
\end{align}$

$1$ of the faces is a $3$ by $7$ rectangle.

>$\begin{align} \text{Area of a rectangle} &=  \text{length} \cdot \text{width}\\\\
&= 3 \cdot 7\\\\
&= {\goldD{21}} \\\\
\end{align}$

$2$ of the faces are triangles. Each triangle has the same base and height.

>$\begin{align} \text{Area of a triangle} &=  \dfrac12 \cdot \text{base} \cdot \text{height}\\\\
&= \dfrac12 \cdot 4 \cdot 3\\\\
&= 6 \\\\
\end{align}$


The total area of these $2$ triangles is $2 \cdot 6 = \maroonD{12}$.

Let's add the areas we found to find the surface area.

>$\begin{align} \text{Surface area} &= \blueD{35}+ \greenD{28} + \goldD{21} + \maroonD{12} \\\\
&= 96\\\\
\end{align}$
**The surface area of this triangular prism is $96$ units$^2$.**

options.alt: A right triangular prism. The bottom face has side lengths of 4 units and 7 units. The side face has side lengths of 3 units and 7 units. The diagonal side face has side lengths of 5 units and 7 units. The triangular prism has a height of 3 units.

A net on a coordinate grid.  The net consists of 3 rectangles and 2 right triangles.  The rectangles are connected by the long side.  The first rectangle has a base of 3 units and a height of 7 units.  The second rectangle has a base of 4 units and a height of 7 units. The third rectangle has a base of 5 units and a height of 7 units.  There is a right triangle connected to the top and bottom of the second rectangle.  Each right triangle has a base of 4 units and a height of 3 units.

assessment_items.question_content:
Check out this square pyramid:


[[☃ image 1]]

**Find the surface area of the square pyramid (above) using its net (below).**  


[[☃ image 2]]

[[☃ input-number 1]] units$^2$
hints:
$1$ of the faces is a square.

>$\begin{align} \text{Area of a square} &=  \text{side} \cdot \text{side}\\\\
&= 5 \cdot 5\\\\
&= {\blueD{25}} \\\\
\end{align}$

$4$ of the faces are triangles. Each triangle has the same base and height.

>$\begin{align} \text{Area of a triangle} &=  \dfrac12 \cdot \text{base} \cdot \text{height}\\\\
&= \dfrac12 \cdot 5 \cdot 4\\\\
&= 10 \\\\
\end{align}$


The total area of these $4$ triangles is $4 \cdot 10 = \greenD{40}$.

Let's add the areas we found to find the surface area.

>$\begin{align} \text{Surface area} &= \blueD{25}+ \greenD{40}\\\\
&= 65\\\\
\end{align}$
**The surface area of this square pyramid is $65$ units$^2$.**

options.alt: A square pyramid.  The side of the pyramid's base is 5 units long and the height of the triangular face measured from the center of the side of base to the apex is 4 units.

A net on a coordinate grid.  The net consists of a square and 4 isosceles triangles. The square has a base of 5 units and a height of 5 units. Each triangle has a base of 5 units and a height of 4 units.

assessment_items.question_content:
Check out this square pyramid:


[[☃ image 1]]

**Find the surface area of the square pyramid (above) using its net (below).**    


[[☃ image 2]]

[[☃ input-number 1]] units$^2$
hints:
$1$ of the faces is a square.

>$\begin{align} \text{Area of a square} &=  \text{side} \cdot \text{side}\\\\
&= 2 \cdot 2\\\\
&= {\blueD{4}} \\\\
\end{align}$

$4$ of the faces are triangles. Each triangle has the same base and height.

>$\begin{align} \text{Area of a triangle} &=  \dfrac12 \cdot \text{base} \cdot \text{height}\\\\
&= \dfrac12 \cdot 2 \cdot 2\\\\
&= 2 \\\\
\end{align}$


The total area of these $4$ triangles is $4 \cdot 2 = \greenD{8}$.

Let's add the areas we found to find the surface area.

>$\begin{align} \text{Surface area} &= \blueD{4}+ \greenD{8}\\\\
&= 12\\\\
\end{align}$
**The surface area of this square pyramid is $12$ units$^2$.**

options.alt: A square pyramid.  The side of the pyramid's base is 2 units long and the height of the triangular face measured from the center of the side of base to the apex is 2 units.

A net on a coordinate grid.  The net consists of a square and 4 isosceles triangles. The square has a base of 2 units and a height of 2 units. Each triangle has a base of 2 units and a height of 2 units.
